content.problemStatement:
An array AAA of length 3N3N3N is said to be balanced if it contains exactly NNN occurrences each of 1,2,1,2,1,2, and 333.

For example, the arrays [1,2,1,3,3,2][1, 2, 1, 3, 3, 2][1,2,1,3,3,2] and [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] are balanced, whereas [1,1,1],[1,2,1,2,1,2],[1, 1, 1], [1, 2, 1, 2, 1, 2],[1,1,1],[1,2,1,2,1,2], and [3,2,1,1,3,3][3, 2, 1, 1, 3, 3][3,2,1,1,3,3] are not.

You are given an integer KKK (1≤K≤1071 \leq K \leq 10^71≤K≤107).

Construct any array AAA of length **at most** 10410^4104, consisting of only the integers 1,2,1,2,1,2, and 333, such that it contains exactly KKK contiguous subarrays that are balanced, i.e. there exists exactly KKK pairs of integers LLL and RRR such that:


1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N
[AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​] is balanced.
It can be proved that a valid array always exists.

Note that you do not need to minimize the length of the constructed array: it only needs to be ≤104\le 10^4≤104.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain a single integer KKK, the target number of balanced subarrays.


Output Format
For each test case, output two lines.



The first line should contain a single integer NNN (1≤N≤1041 \le N \le 10^41≤N≤104), the length of the array you found.
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ (1≤Ai≤31 \le A_i \le 31≤Ai​≤3), denoting the array elements.

If there are multiple possible solutions, any of them will be accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The only subarray of [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] which is balanced, is the whole array itself.
Test Case 2 : The subarrays corresponding to (L,R)=(2,4)(L, R) = (2, 4)(L,R)=(2,4) and (L,R)=(3,5)(L, R) = (3, 5)(L,R)=(3,5) are [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] and [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1]. They are the only 222 balanced subarrays.
